data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-a8718bec">
    <title>cost: charge reviewer cost to cap; review-cost-deferred policy</title>
    <description>
Today reviewer cost is silently undercounted (the review path doesn't accumulate CostUSD into --max-cost). CostUSD is ALREADY reported by Fizeau on Final events for every call (no upstream change needed — it's been there). Fix: charge reviewer cost to --max-cost on the same accumulator the implementer uses. Policy: when implementer fits but implementer+reviewer would exceed, defer review (emit 'review-cost-deferred' event) rather than silently skip.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. --max-cost includes reviewer cost in the accumulator. 2. review-cost-deferred event when budget would be exceeded by review. 3. Tests cover the deferral case + the normal fit case.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:cost, kind:fix</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="cb4a0e18d5e1e54e8a0a44d2854d0c06dfd9b2b0" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T092548-e098b745/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `b6ba4f68` (`fix(cost): charge reviewer cost to max-cost and defer over-budget review [ddx-a8718bec]`).

What changed:
- Reviewer final `CostUSD` is now captured on [`ReviewResult`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go#L72).
- The execute-loop runtime now carries a shared review cost tracker in [`ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go#L32).
- `RunPostMergeReview` adds reviewer cost to that same tracker and emits `review-cost-deferred` when the cap is crossed in [`execute_bead_post_review.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go#L185).
- The CLI and server worker paths now thread the shared tracker through in [`agent_cmd.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go#L1903) and [`workers.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/internal/server/workers.go#L752).
- Added regression coverage for the normal-fit and over-budget cases in [`execute_bead_review_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a8718bec-20260505T092548-e098b745/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go#L123), plus a direct check that reviewer `CostUSD` is preserved from the final event in the same file.

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/agent` passed.
- `go test ./cmd -run 'TestExecuteLoopCostCap_'` passed.
- `go test ./internal/server -run 'TestDoesNotExist'` passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.
- The repo-wide `go-test` hook in the commit also reported unrelated pre-existing failures in cmd installation acceptance tests and one agent integration test; those are not from this change.
